title: Hattie the Mouse's Hat Workshop
summary: Hattie the Mouse is a skilled hat-maker who operates a bustling workshop filled with materials like feathers, paper, and cardboard. Her friend Victor visits to find a new hat but is unsure which style suits him best. Hattie showcases a range of options, from tiny red caps to towering yellow feather hats that nearly hit the ceiling. Victor eventually chooses a large blue cardboard hat adorned with elephant stamps, which perfectly matches his jacket. He loves the design so much that he returns to request a second identical hat, leading Hattie to craft a custom blue-on-blue masterpiece using special star, moon, and sun stamps.

highlightQuote.text: It has elephants on it, and you can take that blue hat home with you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. Now we turn out the lights and the stars are on. So now it's time for...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stars, stories, brio neo.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Brio neo, what does that mean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That means nothing! Brrrrt.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay stuffies, okay Izzy. Come sit with Daddy and we'll do stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hello Dino. Come and sit on my left leg.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. What will tonight's Stars and Stories story be about?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie the mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Hattie the mouse. And what's she doing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie making hats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie making hats. And how should we start our story?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: First, she was going to make a hat for Victor.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was making a hat for Victor. Okay, do you want to tell the story?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I don't want to.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, should I tell it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You should tell it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oscar.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, hi Oscar.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, Oscar. Okay, I've got some new pictures on my lap.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can see Avery's house on your knee.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay, perfect. Well, everybody's on my lap that's cozy.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, you can tell the story with us.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, we'll all tell it together. Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was in her workshop with her friend Victor the mouse. And they were talking about what kind of a hat Victor might like. And Hattie the mouse said, 'You know Victor, I could make you any kind of hat you...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, do you have a scratch on this thumb?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I don't think so.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I see a little scrape up here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, maybe I have a little scrape on my thumb, you're right.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe you need a Band-Aid.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I don't think I do, but thank you. I think it's healing already.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And she said, 'Victor, I could make you a small hat, or a big hat, or a medium hat. I could make you...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait my nose, Daddy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You need a nose wipe? Okay. Well, I have a lot of stuffies on my lap that I need to move first. Okay. And then could you get up and just for a second?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I could.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Whoops. Okay. There's your nose wipe. Do you need another one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. All done? All right. Let's continue.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: All done.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sit lap. Sit house. Sit here. Now I have to sit so...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Nice and cozy. You want to start over our story again?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was sitting in her workshop with Victor mouse and they were talking about what kind of a hat Hattie could make for Victor. And she said, 'You know, I could make you a teeny tiny hat or an enormous hat. I could make you a hat out of Legos or out of blocks or out of paper or cardboard or fur or feathers...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or anything!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did Victor say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Victor said, 'I don't know what kind of a hat I would like.' And Hattie mouse said, 'Well, that's okay. How about we try a few things and we can see what you like. Now here, for instance, is a tiny red hat made out of paper. I'll just pop it on your head and you can see if you like it. Just look in the mirror Victor, do you like this tiny red hat?' And what did Victor say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He liked it? Okay. Well...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Hattie had a red hat too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie wears a tall pointy red hat, that's right. And she said, 'Well, just a minute. Let's try another one and see if you like it even better. Um, this is a tall swirly yellow hat made out of feathers. Do you want to try this one on?' And what did Victor say to that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now for my next one, I have a blue hat too, said Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Victor said, 'Oh, a blue hat? I'll try that one on.' But first try the yellow one on, Victor. 'Oh my,' he said, 'it's so tall. I'm afraid if I walk through a doorway I'll hit my little bump on the ceiling.' And Hattie mouse said, 'That's true. That's more of an outdoor hat since it's so tall.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Victor, you'll have to take the hat off and try the red one. It's not mouse feathers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yes, the red one is made of paper, I see, said Victor. And he put the tiny red hat on. He said, 'But it's so small. I'd like one that's a little bigger.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Try the blue one. It's big!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the big blue hat? Tell me more about this blue hat, said Victor.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It has elephants on it, and you can take that blue hat home with you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Victor said, 'It's a blue hat with elephants on it. What's this made of, Hattie?' And what did she say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's made of cardboard.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's cardboard. Wow. I do like it. Let me try it on, said Victor. And he popped it on his head and then he looked at himself in the mirror and said, 'Oh my, yes, I think it suits me very well. Um, and it even matches the blue color in my jacket.' And Victor said, 'Well, Hattie, I think I've made my choice. I like all of the hats very much, but this blue one with the elephants on it especially. I'll take it!' And what did Hattie mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay. You can take it too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Victor mouse said, 'Thank you.' And he left wearing his blue elephant hat and was very happy. And what happened next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Victor came back again with his elephant hat!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He came back again! And now this time, what did he say to Hattie the mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do you have other hats for me?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And she said, 'Oh yes, I do have other hats for you. What are you interested in, Victor?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Victor said, 'I'm just interested at the blue hat with elephants on it.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But Hattie said, 'Oh, you already have that one. Should I make you another one just like it so you have two?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie mouse said, 'Well, okay, I don't happen to have one on hand, but um, I'll make it for you right now.' And how did she make the hat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Very after she did in my lap instead.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Oscar's in your lap, that's silly. And so Hattie found um some cardboard that was just the right shape and size and she cut and folded it and taped it together and then she got out her paints. What color paints did she use?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She used blue and some elephant paint stamps and maybe some star ones too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, some elephant stamps and some star stamps.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And maybe some moon stamps and sun stamps.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And moon stamps and sun stamps. And space stamps.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And space stamps, yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Base stamps, yeah. And what color did she um make the stamps?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Blue!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Blue? Oh, it was blue on blue. So first she painted the cardboard hat all in blue with her brush and then she collected all those stamps—the elephant stamps and also the sun and moon and stars and space stamps—and she stamped them all over the hat. And then she let it dry a little bit and she said, 'Victor, here it is.' And what did he say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Thank you. Now I have two!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Now I have two elephant hats,' said Victor. 'Well, that's great.' But he could only wear one at a time, so he put on one blue hat and carried the other and walked out of the shop whistling a tune.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Door came back again!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Victor came back to Hattie the mouse's shop again! And what did he say this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This time, can I take off my blue hat and try another hat?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie the mouse said, 'Of course you could try another hat on. But I see we're out of time for tonight's Stars and Stories because our blue timer is up.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But hat up.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So that means...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But Victor still at the Hattie workshop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, so we can continue another time.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Daddy?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Brio neo, brio neo.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Victor the Mouse
characters.description: A mouse searching for the perfect headwear, who becomes fond of blue hats decorated with elephant stamps.

characters.name: Oscar
characters.description: Hunkered in velvety shadows beside one of Mistress Mouse’s tattered stuffy mice, Lily’s brother illuminates a salvaged television with a scavenged flashlight, contentedly nibbling on oily debris. His focus drifts from the flickering screen to the miniature green car mired in a mud puddle—a casualty of the same reckless speed he employs when racing his yellow school bus. Between these high-stakes heats, he engineers sturdy Lego bridges that arch over the surrounding wreckage, all under the unblinking watch of his glass-eyed, plush sentinel.

characters.name: Dino
characters.description: A dinosaur companion who sits on the storyteller's leg.

characters.name: Avery
characters.description: Now christened [Name], this diminutive mole is the newest addition to Izzy’s family and Hattie’s velvety baby sister. A tiny voyager often depicted in portraits wearing oversized clothes, she is a slumbering resident aboard the ship, finding bliss in milk-filled dreams within her cozy "house" upon the storyteller’s knee. To protect [Name]’s fragile rest, the crew maintains a state of hushed reverence, ensuring the vessel remains a quiet sanctuary for its smallest, most loyal passenger.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.

places.name: The Knee House
places.description: A fictional location situated on a knee, belonging to Avery.
